name: claude-usage
description: A local dashboard for tracking your Claude Code token usage, costs, and session history. Pro and Max subscribers get a progress bar. This gives you the full picture.
readme_summary: This Python repository provides a local dashboard to visualize and track your Claude Code token usage, session history, and estimated costs. It works by parsing local log files generated by Claude Code, offering insights beyond what the official UI provides, and is compatible with API, Pro, and Max plans.
key_features: Tracks token usage (input, output, cache)
Logs session history, models, and projects
Estimates costs based on usage
Provides a web-based dashboard with charts
Works with API, Pro, and Max plans
Supports Claude Code CLI, VS Code extension, and Dispatched Code sessions
Incremental scanning for efficiency
No third-party Python packages required
primary_use_case: To provide users with a local, detailed dashboard for monitoring and analyzing their Claude Code token usage and associated costs.
cover_image_prompt: A diligent librarian meticulously organizing and cataloging glowing scrolls, each representing a 'Claude Code' session. The librarian is using a magnifying glass to examine the token counts and cost data inscribed on the scrolls, while a large, clear dashboard displaying charts and graphs of usage trends is projected on the wall behind them. Subtle digital particles representing data flow around the scene, and a small, stylized 'Claude Code' logo is visible on the librarian's desk. The overall mood is one of clarity and insight, with warm, focused lighting. The art style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, modern aesthetic.

name: metrics
description: 📊 An infographics generator with 30+ plugins and 300+ options to display stats about your GitHub account and render them as SVG, Markdown, PDF or JSON!
readme_summary: Metrics is a JavaScript-based tool that generates customizable infographics displaying GitHub account statistics. It offers over 30 plugins and 300 options to visualize data such as contributions, languages, and stargazers, rendering them as SVG, Markdown, PDF, or JSON.
key_features: Infographics generator for GitHub statistics
30+ plugins and 335+ options for customization
Supports users, organizations, and repositories
Renders metrics as SVG, Markdown, PDF, or JSON
Embeddable in GitHub profile readmes
Plugins for commit calendar, languages activity, stargazers, lines of code, and more
primary_use_case: To create and display personalized, visually appealing statistics about a GitHub user's activity and contributions directly within their profile README or other web content.
cover_image_prompt: A digital artisan, akin to a master cartographer, meticulously crafting a vibrant, multi-layered map. Instead of landmasses, the map displays glowing data points representing GitHub commits, languages, and contributions. The artisan uses specialized tools that resemble code snippets and UI elements to draw connections and highlight key areas. The input is a stream of abstract digital data flowing from a stylized GitHub Octocat icon, and the output is the finished, intricate map displayed on a large, illuminated parchment. The scene is set in a clean, modern workshop with subtle glowing circuit patterns on the walls, and the overall mood is one of creative productivity. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clear iconography and a rich, data-driven color palette.

name: open-wearables
description: Self-hosted platform to unify wearable health data through one AI-ready API.
readme_summary: Open Wearables is an open-source, self-hosted platform designed to unify health data from various wearable devices into a single, AI-ready API. It simplifies the process for developers and individuals to access, manage, and derive insights from their health metrics, offering a unified interface instead of managing multiple device-specific integrations.
key_features: Unified API for multiple wearable providers
Normalized health data access (heart rate, sleep, activity, etc.)
Self-hosted solution for data control and privacy
Simplified integration for health applications
AI-powered health insights and automations (upcoming)
Embeddable widgets for applications (upcoming)
primary_use_case: To provide a unified API and self-hosted platform for developers and individuals to aggregate, manage, and analyze health data from diverse wearable devices, enabling faster development of health applications and personalized health insights.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, ancient librarian standing at the center of a vast, circular library. Instead of books, the shelves are filled with glowing, interconnected orbs, each representing data from a different wearable device (e.g., a smartwatch, a fitness tracker). The librarian is holding a single, illuminated scroll that displays a unified, clean dashboard of health metrics like heart rate, sleep patterns, and activity levels. Subtle digital particles flow from the orbs towards the scroll, symbolizing data unification. Small, holographic screens around the librarian show simplified charts and natural language prompts. The overall mood is one of organized knowledge and accessible insight, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a warm, inviting color palette and soft, ethereal lighting.

name: nano11
description: Script to build possibly the most trimmed-down Windows 11 experience.
readme_summary: nano11 is a PowerShell script that automates the creation of a highly streamlined Windows 11 ISO image. It aggressively removes non-essential components, apps, and services to achieve a significantly smaller footprint, making it suitable for testing, development, or embedded use cases in virtual machines.
key_features: Automates the build of a streamlined Windows 11 image using PowerShell.
Utilizes only built-in DISM capabilities and oscdimg.exe.
Creates a bootable ISO with no external binaries.
Includes an unattended answer file to bypass Microsoft Account requirement and enable compact installation.
Aggressively removes bloatware, core system components (like WinSxS, Windows Update, Defender), drivers, and most services.
Results in an image up to 3 times smaller than standard Windows 11.
The resulting OS is not serviceable (no updates, language, or driver additions).
primary_use_case: Creating a minimal, static Windows 11 environment for testing, development, or embedded use in virtual machines.
cover_image_prompt: A minimalist, almost skeletal, Windows 11 logo being meticulously sculpted by a precision laser tool, chipping away excess material to reveal a compact, streamlined core. The 'excess' material, represented by colorful digital debris and discarded app icons, floats away. In the background, a small, clean workbench displays a single, glowing PowerShell script icon and a tiny, bootable USB drive. The overall mood is one of extreme efficiency and reduction, emphasizing the creation of a 'bare-bones' operating system. The style should be a clean, 3D isometric illustration with a focus on sharp lines and a limited, cool color palette.

name: browserytools
description: 136+ free browser-based tools for productivity & developers. 100% client-side — no servers, no uploads, full privacy. Image & on-device AI tools, file/format converters, code formatters, calculators, generators, and more.
readme_summary: BrowseryTools is a comprehensive suite of over 136 free, browser-based tools designed for productivity and developers. All tools operate entirely client-side, ensuring user privacy and eliminating the need for server uploads. The collection includes image manipulation, file conversion, code formatting, AI-powered on-device utilities, and more.
key_features: 136+ free browser-based tools
100% client-side operation (no servers, no uploads, full privacy)
On-device AI tools (image upscaling, captioning, object cutout, transcription, translation, summarization)
Image manipulation (background removal, compression, format conversion, mockups, censoring, collage, resizer)
File and format converters
Code formatters and generators
Calculators
Built with Next.js, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS
primary_use_case: To provide a wide array of free, privacy-focused, client-side tools for everyday productivity tasks and developer utilities directly within the web browser.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, open toolbox overflowing with diverse digital widgets and icons, each representing a different utility. A friendly, anthropomorphic browser character is actively using a magnifying glass to inspect a glowing image, while another hand reaches out to grab a data stream transforming into code snippets. Subtle UI elements and digital particles emanate from the tools, hinting at their functionality. The background is a clean, abstract digital landscape with the 'BrowseryTools' name subtly integrated. The overall mood is helpful and empowering, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with bright, inviting colors and clear visual metaphors for each tool's function.
